result.summary: Autodesk exposes APS HTTP APIs (Authentication and Data Management), GraphQL APIs (AEC Data Model and Manufacturing Data Model), and an Autodesk Product Help MCP server; authenticate with an APS OAuth app (client ID/secret for 2-legged or 3-legged bearer tokens), with Secure Service Account JWTs also available for some server-to-server flows, while MCP auth is currently unknown.

aps_oauth_app.label: APS OAuth application (client ID and client secret, used for 2-legged and 3-legged OAuth)
aps_oauth_app.setup: Create an app in [Autodesk Platform Services](https://aps.autodesk.com/en/docs/oauth/v2/tutorials/create-app). In the APS app portal, register your application to get a `client_id` and `client_secret`, and configure redirect URIs/scopes as needed for 3-legged flows. Use the same app credentials to obtain either server-to-server (2-legged) or user-delegated (3-legged) access tokens through the [OAuth v2 docs](https://aps.autodesk.com/en/docs/oauth/v2/overview/).

aps_ssa_jwt.label: APS Secure Service Account JWT assertion
aps_ssa_jwt.setup: First create a Secure Service Account using the APS app you registered, then generate an RSA key pair for that service account via the [Secure Service Account API](https://aps.autodesk.com/blog/secure-service-account-openapi-spec-here) or its [OpenAPI spec](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/autodesk-platform-services/aps-sdk-openapi/main/secureserviceaccount/secureServiceAccount.yaml). Sign a JWT assertion with the service account's private key, then exchange that JWT for a user-context access token as described in the SSA docs/blog. This credential path is available only to server-to-server APS applications.
